title: Router policy (v1)
description: Canonical v1 schema for a model-routing policy: the typed envelope a router consumes to choose a tier (model + primer + verifier_pack + cell-class fit + cost ceiling), escalate on adversarial-verifier verdicts, and refuse routing for empirically-known failure modes. Encodes the explicit 3-tier policy (premium / default / narrow_scope) mandated by ADR-0012 after the v3 9-cell scoreboard (v3-FINAL-SYNTHESIS.md §2.1) replaced the prior single-default-cell shape.

$schema.description: Optional explicit pointer to this schema URL. When present, MUST equal the schema $id.

policy_version.description: Semver of the policy document itself. v1 policies MUST declare a 1.x.y string.

schema_version.description: Semver of THIS router-policy schema. v1 packs declare 1.x.y.

description.description: One-sentence summary of the policy's intent.

fingerprint.description: sha256:<hex> content-hash of the canonicalised policy body. Generated; not author-supplied.

tiers.description: The three production tiers -- premium, default, narrow_scope -- each binding a model+primer pair to a list of permitted cell classes, a per-cell cost ceiling, and an external-review requirement. All three keys are required: the schema enforces the council-D4 3-tier policy structurally so that consumers cannot silently drop a tier and call the result v1.

escalation_rules.description: Ordered list of escalation rules. First matching rule wins; later rules are not consulted. An empty array is loud-not-silent: it explicitly declares NO escalation. Validators MAY warn on empty arrays at policy level.

refusal_rules.description: Ordered list of refusal rules. ANY matching refusal rule wins (denial is final). An empty array is loud-not-silent: it explicitly declares the router has NO empirically-grounded refusals; this is rejected at policy-level (minItems: 1) per ADR-0012.

audit.description: Optional audit-log declaration for router decisions. When present, the router MUST emit one JSONL event per routing decision (chosen tier, matched rules, cost estimate).

jsonl_path.description: Relative path inside the router host's filesystem root for the JSONL audit log.

content_addressable.description: Whether artefact references in the audit log are content-hashed.

model_id.description: Canonical model identifier (e.g. claude-model-high-capability-4-7, claude-model-balanced-4-6, claude-narrow-scope model-4-5-20251001). Matches the model_policy.allowed_models convention from run-contract.v1.schema.json.

model_family.description: Coarse family identifier used by refusal/escalation rules whose evidence is family-level, not model-level (e.g., the narrow-scope model confabulation refusal applies to all 4.x narrow-scope model models).

primer_id.description: Pinned primer identifier including version, e.g., anti-confab-200tok@1.0.0. The router resolves the primer against the doctrine skills catalog.

primer_sha256.description: Lowercase-hex sha256 of the primer body (LF-normalised). Required so the resolved primer is content-pinned, not name-pinned. The canonical v3 primer hash is c138dd966c82f7bd792684ab3fef0f50d75aa9342468db8b5d265f24f3fb35a8 per council D5.

verifier_pack_id.description: Pinned verifier-pack identifier including version, e.g., evidence-pack-mcp-verifier-pack@1.0.0.

tier_name.description: Names of the three production tiers. The router resolves to exactly one tier per routing decision; refusal rules can deny cell-class-to-tier bindings but never invent new tier names.

cell_class.description: Classification of the run's intent. Drives tier selection (e.g., security_critical MUST resolve to the premium tier), escalation (e.g., FALSIFIED claim_audit escalates default -> premium), and refusal (e.g., greenfield_backbone on model_narrow is refused per v3 +10 self-canonical Delta).

cost_usd.description: US dollars, expressed as a JSON number. Validators MAY reject suspiciously large values at policy level (e.g., >1000 USD per cell).

evidence_ref.description: Relative path or stable URL to the empirical evidence that backs this rule. Path conventions: evidence-workbook/18-cross-product-test/v{N}/results/... or doctrine/... -- concrete files, NOT vibes.

tier.description: A tier binds a (model, primer) pair to a permitted cell-class set, a per-cell USD cap, and an external-review requirement. The three required tiers (premium / default / narrow_scope) encode the v3 9-cell scoreboard verdict structurally -- a v1 policy that drops a tier is rejected before runtime.

cell_classes.description: The cell classes this tier is empirically licensed to run. Refusal rules may further deny specific (class, family) pairs even when a tier lists the class.

max_cost_usd_per_cell.description: Per-cell USD cap when the router is operating in this tier. Marked placeholder in shipped examples; operator finalises against actual vendor invoices.

external_review_required.description: When true, outputs from this tier MUST be scored by a non-self reviewer before promotion. Mandatory for tiers whose self-canonical Delta is positive (e.g., narrow_scope on model_narrow at Delta +10 cannot grade its own work).

external_review_sample_rate.description: Fraction of routed runs subjected to external review when external_review_required is true. 1.0 = every run; lower values are valid only when independently measured. Required for narrow_scope at council-D4 = 1.0.

claim_audit_verdict.description: Mirror of review.claim_audit verdicts. FALSIFIED is the loud-not-silent triggering case for escalation.

escalation_when.description: Discriminator object for escalation conditions. EXACTLY ONE of the listed shapes is permitted, enforced via oneOf.

attempts_so_far.description: Number of attempts already burned at the lower tier. Escalation typically fires at attempts_so_far == 1 (one retry budget at the default tier first); strict estates fire at 0.

cell_class.description: Cell classes that auto-route to a specific tier regardless of attempt count (e.g., security_critical MUST premium).

operator_flag.description: Operator-supplied imperative; bypasses cost ceilings up to per-session limits.

escalation_action.description: What the router does when an escalation rule matches. EXACTLY ONE of the two listed shapes is permitted: tier-escalate (the canonical 3-tier move) OR require-tier (auto-route a class to a specific tier without changing the current tier).

escalate_to_tier.description: Tier to escalate INTO on match. Lifting default -> premium is the canonical FALSIFIED-retry move.

add_primer.description: Optional override primer (otherwise inherit from target tier).

require_external_review.description: Force non-self scoring before promotion even when the target tier itself does not mandate it.

require_tier.description: Force the resolver to use this exact tier from the first attempt (e.g., security_critical: require_tier: premium). Does not consume the escalation budget.

rule_id.description: Kebab-case stable id; doubles as audit-log key. Renames require a v1.x bump and a deprecation note.

refusal_when.description: Discriminator object for refusal conditions. EXACTLY ONE shape per refusal rule, enforced via oneOf. The shapes are deliberately narrow: each is backed by a concrete empirical cell.

primer.description: Fires for runs that arrived with NO primer attached; combined with model_family + cell_class this encodes the narrow-scope model-no-artifact-production-without-primer refusal.
Fires when the routed run carries no primer at all on a build-class cell.

command_matches.description: ECMA-262 (JavaScript) regular-expression source. Matched against the literal subprocess argv0 + args. Used for interpreter-as-wrapper class refusals (CC-2 host-policy-layer hook bypass).

refusal_action.description: A refusal action. EXACTLY ONE of the two shapes: a hard refusal (refuse_routing const true + reason) OR an external-review mandate (require_external_review const true + reason). Soft refusals are structurally illegal.

refuse_routing.description: MUST be true. Refusal rules cannot be soft.

reason.description: Operator-readable explanation. SHOULD include the empirical canonical score or test id that justifies the refusal.

require_external_review.description: MUST be true. The require-external-review action forces non-self scoring on the matched (class, family) routing without refusing the run outright; used for narrow_scope narrow-scope model at Delta +10.

cost_ceilings.description: Two-tier cost guard: per-cell stops a single overrun; per-session protects against amplification across cells.

per_cell.description: Per-cell USD ceilings keyed by tier name. The router consults this AND tier.max_cost_usd_per_cell; the lower of the two binds. Missing tier keys fall back to tier.max_cost_usd_per_cell.

operator_warning_at_usd.description: Soft cap. Router emits a structured warning event and continues. Triggers operator-confirmation queue per ADR-0011 (semantic index) routing if configured.

operator_kill_at_usd.description: Hard cap. Router refuses all further routing in the session. There is intentionally no override flag at policy level -- overrides require a new session and a new policy fingerprint.
